q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Sink is _os.system("ping -c 1 " + host) on line 127, which is direct shell execution with a user-controlled argument.

q2_observable_after_return.evidence: Not evaluated; decision terminated at Q1.

q3_result_in_memory.evidence: Not evaluated; decision terminated at Q1.

mock_guidance.target: Mock _os.system (or os.system) to prevent actual shell execution.
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full command string argument passed to os.system, specifically the "ping -c 1 " + host concatenation, so the test can assert the injected payload is present.
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return 0 to simulate a successful command exit status, allowing the function to continue and return {'status': 0} normally.
notes: The function returns {'status': status}, where status is likely the return value of os.system, so the mock must return an integer.
Ensure the mock captures the argument before any execution; do not call the real os.system even with safe-looking inputs.
